title: Silver and its compounds - Canada.ca
content:
## Silver and its compounds
- Silver and its compounds
These substances were identified for action under the Chemicals Management Plan (CMP). This screening assessment focuses on the silver moiety and therefore considers silver in its elemental form, silver-containing substances, and all forms of silver found in the environment. The health conclusion includes, but is not limited to the 7 substances identified in the table below (referred to in the screening assessment as the Silver and its Compounds Group); however, the proposed ecological conclusion is limited to those 7 substances.
- Summary of publications
- Follow-up activities
Public comments on the draft screening assessment
Final screening assessment
Proposed Conclusion on section 64 criteria
Follow-up activities
Silver and its compounds
Nitric acid silver(1+) salt
Silver chloride (AgCl)
Silver bromide (AgBr)
Sulfuric acid, disilver(1+) salt
Silver sulfide (Ag 2 S)

### Follow-up activities
Although a risk to human health or the environment has not been identified at levels of exposure considered in the screening assessment, there may be a concern if exposure to silver and its compounds were to increase. As a result, silver and its compounds may be considered in future initiatives to obtain additional information.
Publication of the final screening assessment. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I, Vol. 156, No. 35 .
Publication and start of 60-day public comment period on the draft screening assessment (including follow-up activities). The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 154,  No. 33 .
